title: Relaxing is great
body: Taken time for myself at night to relax, meditate, breathing exercises, in writing down my worries, and my gratitudes has really helped me a lot. I feel like I’m on the right track.

title: Subscription Unreasonable
body: If you asked me a month ago I would give this app four stars if not five, but then I realized that after six months the real daily tools/content conclude but they still keep charging you a subscription fee. It’s like paying for a cheeseburger but the restaurant keeps sending you a bill for the same amount that the cheeseburger originally cost every month because you digested it and its molecules are still being used in your cells. Then if you stop paying, they come and slice off part of your fingertip to reclaim their property. If you stop paying then you’re cut off from all of the tools you used such as bookmarking certain passages and journal entries. How can people that design such an effective tool then turn around and be so, so greedy? Gross.

title: Stellar Sleep Is Stellar!
body:
When I bought the app, I was doubtful that my sleep would actually improve, I don’t sleep through the night like I’d hoped but I awaken refreshed and ready for the day bc of the advice and encouragement the app provided on a daily basis. I understand my habits much better and I get more deep sleep and REM sleep than I did. 
BEST OF ALL, they don’t cut you off at the end of the time you pay for. You get an extra 30 days that helped me immensely! I have saved many “modules” to help me cope better. Now that the extra 30 days are over, I can keep up with the app for nothing! 5 star? If I could give it 10, I would.

title: Customer Service
body: Excellent customer service

title: Works when you use it
body: I love this app have used it several times now and just signed up for another month

title: App wont cancel
body: I signed up to try the app but did not find a lot of value so I tried to cancel it but they billed me $188 anyway despite being in the seven day cancellation Window. Impossible to find a telephone number.

title: Worked for Post-Menopausal Insomniac
body: I’ve grappled with insomnia for nearly a decade. I wondered if CBT-I could work for insomnia in menopausal woman. It DOES! The secret for me has been revisiting it regularly. My fitful, unrestorative sleep and 3 a.m.  awakenings began in perimenopause nearly a decade ago. I was under no illusion that I would be “cured” in a matter of weeks. When I stick to the program, I sleep better. When I do not sleep well, I now have the skills to bounce back quickly. After trying countless medications, supplements (including magnesium), and hormones, this app has helped me the most by far. I am eternally grateful.

title: It works if you do
body: This is my second time through the program. The first go around worked well until I had another major life event that caused me to lose sleep again. Now, I’m already sleeping better and I’m only a week into the program. This is definitely worth the few minutes a day you’ll spend on it!

title: Sleep
body: Charged me 188 dollars after my free trial was over and had to get a new debt card do not recommend using this app

title: Costs $188/3 months
body: They have you complete a long signup form just to reveal the price at the end of $188 every 3 months. They even let you pick how much you wanna pay for a 1 week trial, telling you that they want to be able to help more people and that you paying more for the trial lets them make the app more accessible to those who need it. In reality this sounds like a lie to guilt you into paying more for a trial.

title: Very helpful
body: This app combines many different techniques to help deal with insomnia. It really helped me during a stressful period when I couldn’t sleep. I recommend it highly!

title: scam trial
body: I cancelled on day 6 of 7 of the trial and the app still attempted two charges of $188. the advice and tips the app gives during the trial are very general and helped relieve some anxiety, but this is all information you can find just searching online.

title: Life changing
body: I have struggled with sleep difficulties for decades. This app has transformed and improved my sleep in just one week. I am grateful to have found it.

title: Some things I wish could be improved on.
body:
If you put 25 hours, they would put like 2 hours instead. This is a glitch.  

The app doesn’t enforce when I should be watching the videos, I didn’t know I was supposed to watch them. So I started a week later than I’m supposed to :(  It would be better if the app reminds me that I need to watch those videos everyday. 

As well as, the program heavily recommends sleep restriction, because I can’t do sleep restriction, I felt left out watching the videos in the second week. I really want to try out sleep restriction, but I have a dog I need to walk everyday and I can’t do that on sleep restriction. I also need to study for my GED, I cant study if I restrict my sleep. 

It was stressful opting out of the sleep restriction program, but it was even more stressful seeing that the videos were centered around sleep restriction.

title: Like a Miracle
body: I suffered for a year, every night I had fear of going to bed. The first night I used the app, it was very comforting and helpful. After 2 weeks I was sleeping better. After 2 months I was cured. Don’t wait, get the app as soon as possible!

title: Fixed my insomnia
body: Thank you to the creators of this app! I was dealing with sudden, debilitating insomnia for 6 months before starting stellar sleep. I was averaging 3-4 hours of sleep per night and was becoming a shell of myself. I questioned the methods originally, but was desperate. Within 2 weeks, I was getting 6 hours of sleep. Within a month, I was getting 7. Now, 4 months out, I’m getting the sleep I was getting before my insomnia began and am sleeping regularly again. I don’t have to think about insomnia anymore. The micro-modules add up and you can feel them slowly make a difference. The sleep restriction was tough, but it was the most critical component to getting regular again. Advice- take a cold shower to help you stay awake, but not within an hour of when you actually need to go to sleep! I no longer need stellar sleep, but I’m forever grateful and will shout my support from the rooftops!

title: Great Program!
body: Stellar Sleep works, helped me resolve my insomnia while giving me tools to employ in future. Thought nothing would work, but this app did!!!!

title: Well done and great customer service
body: I was suffering from insomnia and I’m also an LMHC.  I really appreciated learning about the issue as well as being equipped with tools to fix it versus just having a fix for the night.  It may take a little bit but it’s worth the effort.  Also had an issue with the subscription and customer service was great!

title: Insomnia conquered
body: After at least 40 years of struggling with insomnia my New Year's resolution for 2025 was to successfully defeat it. I had tried pretty much everything imaginable up until now. After starting the Stellar Sleep app I knew there was hope for me. In the first 10 days I was already getting back to a natural sleeping pattern. I have been hopelessly addicted to an afternoon nap and did not think I could survive without it, but when my quality of sleep at night improved my need for a nap was greatly lessened. I still get sleepy in the afternoons and evenings, but it feels like it is natural rather than a need to catch up on lost sleep. I am motivated to push through it for the sake of a good solid eight hours of sleep. If I can do it, anyone can! So thrilled with this app!

title: Really works
body: I was on a medicine that deprived me of sleep for over a year. I have never slept well since. NOW, with Stellar Sleep, I am actually having consecutive nights of good sleep.

title: Tried it for the 7 day trial period
body: I tried it for the 7 days didn't see any progress so I tried to cancel it but they had already charged my card. I disputed it and stellar would not refund me the $188. Absolute trash company do not get yourself mixed up with them!

title: Pay wall
body: They don’t tell you before you download that the trial is as low as $0.99 but then it’s $188 for 3 months.  I desperately need help sleeping but I can’t afford that.

title: If you don’t like cartoons lecturing you, avoid this app AND ITS NOT REALLY A FREE TRIAL
body:
I found the cartoons insulting to my  intelligence. If you are an adult and like to be treated like an adult, this is not for you. 

The “free trial” is 7 days, but they arent going to give you any specific advice until DAY EIGHT. 

You see, they need seven days of data first to “calibrate”. Of course, you can import data from the health app, so this doesn’t seem true. 

In the meantime, you can watch cartoons that will give you info you can get anywhere. 

If you want any actual help from this app, you’re paying the full price. That’s unethical. 

Between that and the cartoons, one star.

title: Fraudulent billing
body:
This app would be great BUT READ THE REVIEWS about fraudulent billing. 

I did the 7-day free trial. Cancelled on day 7. Come to find out that 3 months later, my account was *somehow* still active when my credit card was charged $188. 

I’d seen in reviews people complaining about this but thought they just didn’t cancel soon enough. Now I see it’s a feature of the app. 

Find another app to coach you on how to change your sleep habits. This one will steal your money.

title: Starting to work!
body:
Just starting the program but I am very hopeful that this could be the answer to the torture of insomnia!  
Grateful to have found Stellar Sleep and I plan on sticking to it!

title: Atomic habits meets CBT-I I’m insanely impressed
body:
Okay initially thought this was a money grab because I’m jaded about all the courses online but this is exceptional. 

They give you 5-10 minutes daily assignments to slowly start cleaning up your sleep hygiene and while not perfect my numbers are improving! 

I have an Oura ring helping me track its efficacy so far so good!

title: Not all that it’s cracked up to be
body: This app has some useful insights, but much of it is really heavily geared towards office workers with regular schedules. Throughout the program this is really apparent in the language and this is quite frustrating for someone with an irregular schedule. If you’re white collar and awake because you can’t shut off, this may help you. If you actually have physical challenges and a schedule that causes havoc on sleep, don’t bother.

title: So bad!
body: The talking owl was a joke! It was also tough to follow along the audios as I’m not an audio person at all. Waste of money - felt like I was just interacting with an AI bot.

title: Great app, Definitely well-researched Stuff in here.
body: Definitely love what they’re doing here, Very organized and Practical stuff within this App. However, App could have more CBT-I or whatever the newer Type of Sleep therapy that they offer, which is the more Mindfulness-based Insomnia help, Yes. Gettin’ out of One’s head is Incredible for easing anxiety/Overall insomnia, Thanks. Love the App’s main Ideas, awesome. Easy to Use features also, Thanks.

title: Helped with Perimenopausal Insomnia
body:
To say this app changed my life might be a bit dramatic but it absolutely changed my sleep, which in turn, changed my life. I suffered from insomnia both when my
son was born and again in perimenopause. It became so bad I would panic at bedtime, knowing I wouldn’t get adequate rest.  I knew it had become mental at this point as well and I needed help.  Stellar helped from Day 1- helping me understand WHY I was panicking and how to use my thoughts to help me relax. Within days, I was sleeping better. Within a week I was stringing together full nights of sleep, which had not happened in over a year. My only regret is not using the app sooner.

title: Outstanding AI
body: Outstanding AI feedback in Worry and Gratitude journal feature.  Helps one actually resolve problems to reduce stress and running mind.

title: Worked brilliantly
body: The program is great. The behavioral portion is informative, helpful, minimal time investment to get great results. The sleep restriction is challenging but incredibly effective. I went from horrible insomnia, using medication frequently, to normal healthy sleep. The support is also extraordinary. I had a real person respond quickly to any questions. Lastly, doing the program with an Oura ring makes it much for streamlined to log and track sleep.

title: Won’t stop charging your CC after you cancel
body: They kept charging my card up to 5x after the trial even after though I canceled the subscription. Made me change my credit card number twice. Not worth the headache and stress. STAY AWAY

title: Canceling is hard, $183 a MONTH charge
body:
The 7 day free trial is NOT worth the cost you might incur, if like me it was hard to cancel. I had to despute a charge after trying to cancel. I still paid for a month of this and disputed a
Second months 183$! Charge. Also there are many CBT-I free and low cost resources online! Don’t get caught out.

title: Worked and Great Customer Service
body: After the app helped me sleep only after two days, I decided to delete it because I didn’t need it anymore. I thought I deleted my subscription but I saw they charged me after my one-week trial was up. I asked for a refund, never really expecting to get one but they refunded me entirely. This made my day and I 100% recommend this app to anyone.

title: So thankful
body: It’s only been a week and my life has changed. The creators of this program truly care about helping people

title: It’ll cause a fraud alert with your credit card company
body: I had subscribed to this 6 months ago and stopped using it. Without warning they renew my subscription which triggers a fraud alert on my credit card. Unbelievable. No email saying hey we’re going to renew. My wife cancels our card thinking it’s fraud.  Horrible. It’s nothing but a poorly thought out rehash of CBTi. The price is too high and their customer service is seriously shady. Stay away.

title: I thank God for this app!
body: I was having severe insomnia and through this app, prayer, major life changes, and focus I was able to restore my sleep. Not only did I learn so much about sleep but I learned a lot about mindset, how our brains work and so much more. 5/5 - don’t hesitate, do it.

title: Ineffective for my sleep issues
body: For me, it’s not working.  I’m getting less sleep than when I started this program three weeks ago.  I think the issue is that this seems to focus on those who have trouble falling asleep.  It does briefly mention those with issues waking up in the middle of the night and not being able to get back to sleep, but only very little.  That’s the problem I have and I’ve found this to be ineffective for that.  Since I’ve started this, I’ve only been able to fall back asleep one time and and am typically getting about 4 hours of sleep a night.  The other concern I have is that this is a very difficult and controversial approach to overcoming insomnia. The central element is sleep restriction therapy. It is absolutely brutal, but you don’t get the details as to just how brutal this approach is until after the low introductory cost for the first week has passed and you’ve been billed a hefty sum for your subscription. My bad for not doing my due diligence before signing up, but I’m pretty worn out and willing to try just about anything.  Maybe it will get better, but so far it is absolutely terrible and I’m much worse off than before I started it. It would have been nice to know just how difficult this was before I paid for the subscription. That being said, from other reviews I’ve read, it does seem to work for other people.  I’m just not one of them.

title: Great program
body: It works just like Noom, giving 3-5 minutes of education per day, and having you log sleep and commitments.  Improved my sleep almost immediately.  Has been a real lifesaver, helping me get rejuvenating sleep without the struggle.

title: Excellent sleep resource
body: This program is fantastic. My hope is that there will be added content for those of us who do shift work. This line of work makes it impossible to have the same bedtime and wake up time everyday. Would love to hear some suggestions.

title: Saved my life
body: I’ve struggled with insomnia for awhile and nothing was working. Melatonin, sleeping pills and THC worked for awhile but stopped. I couldn’t fall asleep no matter what I took it and I was  getting scared.  I couldn’t sleep for days and even when I did sleep it was for maybe 4 hours. I finally went to my new doctor, I had switched doctors because the last one really didn’t address why I couldn’t sleep and prescribed meds that didn’t work, the new dr diagnosed me with anxiety due to being a caregiver for my husband fighting stage 4 cancer and was prescribed medication for anxiety and Temazepam for sleep, which usually worked, but I didn’t want to get addicted.  The App works extremely well if you follow the program. It can be difficult but keep trying. You’ll get there. I’m still taking my anxiety meds but rarely need Temazepam.  I highly recommend this app.

title: BEWARE: TEHY STEAK YOUR MONEY WITH NO AUTHORIZATION
body: I canceled the app after the trial only to see 4 CHARGES from an account that I did not even link to the app!! These app developers are scammers. I ah e submitted several tickets and am not hearing back from them.

title: 15 years of insomnia… cured in a month
body: This program saved my life. If you suffer from insomnia, the cost of this program is dirt cheap compared to the benefits. I was skeptical at first but decided to give it a try after not being able to find a CBT-I therapist in town, and became extremely desperate to sleep. I was also in regular therapy to address my problems with anxiety, so please know what most sleep issues are related to anxiety, but I needed the sleep restriction because I had developed a severe fear of my bed at some point and needed it addressed. This program was a life saver and I recommend it to everyone

title: About the app
body:
I think it’s been helpful but I feel it’s generic 
Not custom to me personally.
When I started I was getting 0 hours of sleep every other night and the only reason I was even getting sleep every other night was because I was taking 100 mg of trazadone and other sleep aids.
The only reason I’ve gotten sleep every night this week is because I’ve been taking trazadone with Nyqil 4 nights in a row.
When I can sleep without drugs I could give a higher rating but I’m hopeful this app will get me there.

title: Combat Veteran Review
body:
I have been dealing with insomnia for years. The Veterans Affairs sleep doctor did something similar and the VA has a free application called CBT-I.  The Stellar Sleep app is different because of the education piece.  

I remember thinking, “why is the VA doctor telling me to only allow myself to sleep in a 5.5 hour window”. I was frustrated with the guidance and only lasted two days. I’d rather just use every supplement known to man at this point. 

Stellar Sleep educates and explains things. It’s essentially automated therapy, but with great communication and tools (e.g. guided progressive meditation) that help. 

By the middle of the second week I stopped taking melatonin and about 10 other supplements. I would fall asleep quickly.  I still struggled to sleep without waking up at 3am, but learned how to fall back asleep within ~10 minutes. The stress of waking up was no longer stressful. Now I’m a month in to the program and I don’t even do anything special to fall asleep if I wake up in the middle of the night. I just fall asleep again. 

The key to this program is commitment to yourself and achieving what you want out of getting better sleep. If you are ready to apply some work to your habits and learn some things to sleep better, get this app. It is well worth the money.

title: So far it’s stuff you probably know already but it’s still worth it
body: Go to bed and wake up at the same time. Don’t stay in bed more than 20 minutes at a time. It’s stuff you’ve probably already heard. But it does provide some much needed structure and helps you track your progress. It’s also nice to have someone acknowledge your sleeping difficulties even if it’s only a cartoon owl. The big kicker for me was the epiphany I had that I shouldn’t use my meds to go to bed whenever I want but take them at the same time every day. You can probably piecemeal together your own strategy but for me at $200 a year it might be worth it. I don’t think $200 for 3 months is a very good deal. So don’t accept that offer if you can get a better one. Full disclosure I’m only 1 week into the program so I don’t know what gems might show up later.

title: Amazing
body: Been struggling with 2-4am wake ups for over 4 years.  Two weeks in and the pattern is significantly different.  The app is well worth the price and could probably be triple the price and still be worth it.  The knowledge and practical education will last a life time.  There is a ton of misconception out there about sleep and this app seems to set a lot of records straight.  Thank you!

title: Game changer
body: The modules were spot on in helping me to understand why I was suffering from insomnia. The recommended solutions were very effective. I saw improved sleep in the first week of use. Well worth the price.

title: Feels like a scam
body: The pricing feels designed to make you lose sleep, not get it.

title: Disappointing
body: Have implemented for >3 weeks with no discernible change in sleep. While I understand that things take time, don’t make promises you can’t keep “sleep reset”. They string you along with a trial, then short lessons with information that could be found on a basic google search. THEN they charge you ridiculous amounts of money to continue without seeing any results and make you pay in advance.  Not worth the time, energy or money.

title: Excellent App
body: Have had periodic bouts of sleeplessness which was causing me to be very anxious about sleep and only perpetuating the problem. The guided sleep journey in this app helps me relax and build good sleep inducing  habits that have helped me squash the sleep anxiety. It is a journey and I feel that I will always have that sleep Anxiety trigger but this app certainly helps me manage and keep those sleep demons at bey!

title: Charged For Subscription After Deleting the App
body: Had a few bug issues with this app and decided to not continue with the annual subscription so I deleted the app only to find that they charged me $131 a few days later. Reached out to customer service and have yet to receive a refund. DO NOT SIGN UP FOR THIS!!

title: What’s up?
body:
I’ve been on board for almost 2 weeks during which most of my activity has been diary. Diary does not help my insomnia. 
The sound recordings are nice and calming but have not changed my sleep pattern/problem.
My efforts to generate sleep drive have possibly been helpful but only intermittently. The only factor that has improved my sleep has been a bad night the night before.
I need help! I want to sleep w/o addictive drugs. While I am taking less and subsequently losing more sleep, I do not see a major change as yet.
Perhaps this comes later. I would like this answer. Do we intervene further into sleep onset  beyond breathing exercises? Meditation? gradititude?
I needs plan! This seems entirely too automated!

title: Disappointing
body:
Do not waste your money. Scant content ladled  out in ponderously small daily doses. I opted out of sleep restriction and was supposed to be given a personalized program.  It was very generic and could be obtained for free off of hundreds of internet sits. The delivery is also silly and the cost of $60+ per month is ridiculous.  

For $59 annually, I subscribed to Bloom , an excellent CBT app that has two different 6-7 day sleep modules that has transformed and largely solved my sleep problems.  It has numerous other mental wellness programs as well on other areas

title: Huge help.
body: Just since starting the program, I’ve had much improved sleep every night but one. First of all getting out of bed, and only using it to sleep and for sex. I was in bed many hours a day and this has helped In addition, I have stopped routine alcohol at bedtime, and that has helped significantly also. I can’t thank you enough for this program I really recommended highly.

title: Try ACT for sleep
body: I wish CBT-i wasn’t promoted as the first line treatment for insomnia. I’m glad I didn’t pay money for this and only did the trial but a hyper focus on sleep, limiting time I had to fall asleep, and trying to create perfect sleep conditions pushed sleep further away than it’s ever been. Using Sleep School based on ACT I learned acceptance and diffusions, something I never would’ve gotten by only sleeping on the couch and counting every minute of sleep to judge myself. I’m happy for the people cbt-i does work for but I wish there was a disclaimer about how this is not for everyone and there is another option.

title: Great course
body: A really good app to help give you the structure to stick to the program.  One big weakness is that it does not sync across devices.

title: Sneaky subscription cancel
body: They charge your $188 a month and when you want to cancel it the app sends you to Paypal and PayPal sends you to their website, where there is no option to cancel the subscription.

title: 10 Years of Insomnia Cured
body:
The program in this app ended a decade long stretch of insomnia. Before finding this I tried everything from supplements (magnesium, melatonin) to prescription medication to lifestyle changes (no caffeine, exercise more, etc) and nothing really worked. Many nights a week I would be up for hours and feel desperately tired the following day. 

After completing this program I fall asleep fast and stay asleep. If I wake up to use the bathroom in the night I am back to sleep quickly. I do not wake up to a spinning mind, I simply sleep at night. It’s miraculous. This is the only thing that has every truly helped me with sleep. I can now say I do not have insomnia. I cannot recommend this app more highly.

title: Thank you so much!
body: This app is keeping me honest!  Very helpful!

title: Program description is not clear
body:
Pros: some good content and sleep tips plus self help guides, quality relaxation guides 

Cons: if you opt out of the restricted sleep program you only get 2 months of impersonal sleep tips, it was marketed as a personal program which it was not, after completing my 2 months of  program I had to email them to find out that absolutely no new content - was told I could review the material from month 2 - this was also not clear and it is a super expensive program - had they allowed a 2 month subscription it would have been more acceptable

title: Definitely not worth it
body: Absolutely terrible app. The sleep restriction window didn’t even change based on my feedback/sleep quality for that week. This technique also ended up making me more anxious and worsening my insomnia. Don’t waste $200 on something you could easily do yourself better

title: I can sleep now
body: A few weeks ago, I was exhausting every option I could to sleep. Without sleep meds I’d get maybe 3 hours, with sleep meds around 5. I was exhausted. My problem was sleep anxiety. I hate feeling forced to go to bed at a certain time to make sure I got those 8 hours that everyone claims you need to function. However stellar sleep was able to help me change my mindset and I can sleep on my own without sleep meds for about 6 and a half hours. Sometimes I take sleep medicine if I get that super bad sleep anxiety. It hasn’t fixed every problem but it’s helped me rewire some harmful thoughts and habits into better ones that allow me to sleep and not focus so much on the little things that kept me awake at night. I’d give the app 5 stars but I cant complete the 30 day sleep challenge for some reason. It won’t log my progress and has me stuck on day one of 30. Even without the challenge though, it has helped tremendously which is why it’s got a 4 of 5.

title: Helpful
body:
Program has been helpful and provides realistic strategies
I just need to commit to them!

title: Insomnia
body: I considered insomnia, a part of my heritage. My mother has insomnia my sister has insomnia, and I thought I would never sleep without any difficulty, but this app is truly show me that I do not have to own that title and I can learn to sleep like I did when I was a child.

title: Customer service issues
body:
I like the app, and decided to try it on a short term basis. When the subscription was set up, the price was not what the customer service rep and I agreed. It was much higher. Upon communicating about this I was told I would have a life time membership.
I was considering the pros and cons of this, but rep was not available for ongoing discussion. My subscription was canceled altogether, not by me. While it had been cancelled the charge to my account remained. Upon reconnecting with rep, the subscription was reinstated and the ticket was closed, ending communication, from their end.
They now have a very dissatisfied customer who cannot recommend this app, due to the business end.

title: No accessibility
body: To charge almost $60 a month and not have closed captioning is criminal.

title: Horrible app
body: Program will not download despite multiple attempts. Sent a message to help desk which has a 3 day turn around which is not helpful when I only have a one week trial which I have already paid for.

title: Beware - Unauthorized Charge
body: I signed up for a one week trial. While the app offered some helpful suggestions it did not go beyond the standard advice for insomnia. I did not want to continue. Then I discovered a $188 unauthorized charge on my credit card. When I signed up for the trial, I specifically chose the option that would discontinue the app unless I made an additional authorization. I immediately contacted customer service. They said they issued a refund but I am still waiting. This app violated the terms of our agreement.

title: I can sleep now!
body: This app has totally changed my approach to solving my severe insomnia. I’ve had an average of 2-3 nights sleep for 7 months, which has left me feeling anxious, depressed, exhausted and in physical pain. I’ve felt as though my proverbial cheese was slipping off my proverbial cracker. This program is very enlightening and easy to follow. Most of it educates the user about things they’d just never know without it. While I did not use the accountability feature, I did value being accountable to myself for healing my own sleep deficit; it is a sort of gentle self-challenge. The sleep badges are adorable and strangely satisfying to earn! One improvement I can think of, is to be able to go back to all of the sleep modules already completed, so the lessons can be revisited if a user wants to. Over all, I highly recommend this app to anyone who struggles with insomnia. I only wish I’d found it 6 months ago!

title: Stellar Sleep has solved insomnia
body:
I used to seriously struggle with sleep, getting 1-2 hours some nights. That feeling of shutting my eyes, awake in bed, sometimes feels worse than a nightmare.

Been using this sleep app for a while now, and it's a game-changer! Stellar Sleep has solved sleep with their psychology method. I consistently get a great night’s sleep and wake up feeling so energized. I no longer need coffee to kickstart my mornings.

What these guys have in this app changes peoples lives and one day will change the world. It's like having a personal sleep expert, for a price that isn’t even comparable. Highly recommend!

title: Probably saved my life
body:
I've been having trouble sleeping for a year and a half now. I've tried many times to get better, but nothing has ever worked. I've tried super rigorous sleep schedules, sleeping pills, exercising to tire myself out, meditation...basically anything that anyone in my life has recommended, but nothing has worked. It's gotten to a point where I can literally feel my heart palpitating because of my lack of sleep. I'll go a few nights with less than 5 hours of sleep, and for the next few morning, my heart will beat like crazy at random points in the middle of the day.

My friend recommended Stellar Sleep to me this January (new year, new year right?), and I've been telling her that she probably saved my life. When you start the program, it asks if you want it to be more advanced or more basic. I chose the advanced option because I knew I had to go all-in if this was going to do anything for my sleep. It also asks you to designated accountability "coaches", who are basically people in your life that will help you stick with the program. After that, Stellar Sleep gave me videos to watch every day and quizzes to complete. It basically slowly taught me how to identify what exactly was going on in my life that was destroying my sleep, and then created a methodical plan to tackling those things one by one. Now, when I open the app and see the owl, I just feel so emotional and happy about all the support I'm getting.

The program itself isn't a quick fix, and takes work for sure, but it works. And for me, that's all I care about. I usually spend about 15-30 minutes a day in the app (I like poking around the different charts), but honestly even if you told me I had to spend an hour a day for the rest of my life using this app to let me sleep again, it would be worth it.

title: Surprisingly effective
body:
For roughly ten years, I've been wrestling with subpar sleep, a byproduct of my demanding career as a corporate attorney in an ever-bustling city.

I realized I needed to take decisive action not merely for personal comfort, but as a career imperative. In a field where precision and mental agility are paramount, a compromised sleep schedule was proving detrimental. I tried various sleep solutions, from noise machines to aromatic oils and even prescription aids. Sadly, each fell short. They either served as distractions or left me with an undesirable mental fog.

Initially, I approached Stellar Sleep with a healthy dose of skepticism. However, the scientifically supported methods the app employed to tackle sleep issues were compelling. The program felt different; it was more akin to a personal sleep coach, educating me on psych techniques to help me discover the deep-seated reasons for my sleep issues.

The program includes their promised psych videos and then also a several tools to help you track your sleep, track your thoughts, and habit tools. It's all very convenient but honestly I also don't exactly love the idea that everything I did to improve my sleep is in the app. What happens if I decide in a year or two that I will stop paying? Does it all disappear?

Nevertheless, the transformation brought about has been nothing short of remarkable. Within weeks, I began experiencing deeper, more consistent sleep. Work stress, which once reliably sabotaged my rest, no longer had the same effect. My sleep became a rejuvenating process, irrespective of the day's pressures.

If I had to pick another gripe (other than the data being locked in), it is that I wish there was more human interaction. Let me talk to someone face to face (on Zoom) or build a forum where I can talk to the other people using the tool. Sleep is lonely, and that would really help.

title: I’m over the moon
body: I’ve had really bad insomnia for over 10 years, I’ve tried seemingly everything under the sun. I happened to stumble across this app and I’ve had a couple one off bad nights here and there but the tools I’ve learned from this app have taken me from average 3 hours a night to pretty consistent 7 hours a night. I can’t believe it, I am so grateful I found this!!!!

title: Worth the money
body: Stellar sleep has finally fixed my long long sleep problems. I have struggled with insomnia since college due to like academic stress, anxiety, and just a lot of social stressors, and I think it really got worse during covid. I’ve tried rain sounds, other random sleep inducing videos on youtube, and of course melatonin everyday but they weren’t all really working. But I think stellar sleep really kind of gave me tools? Skills? To manage sleep and build habits that I didnt know I needed. I know it feels pricey compared to all those other sleep related apps there but I really think this is the one that works. Give it a try everyone !!!!

title: Be careful, auto renew charged me $130 every three months!
body:
This app has been draining my bank account of $130 every 3 months with no email reminder. It’s definitely not worth that. I can see a sleep expert for less than that. 

Customer service is non existent. I’ve submitted a ticket for a refund of $260 and am waiting to hear back.

title: Misleading
body: Getting into the app it suggests a $9/week cost. Then PayPal informs me that I now have a subscription that costs $188 every three months. They need to be honest up front about this very high price.

title: Beware this app!
body:
UPDATE: I wrote this review 7.5 months ago and the developer just NOW responded to my review. That’s….some customer service. Shading billing practices and lackluster customer service combined with an unhelpful app. Avoid. 

This app does not appear on your subscriptions page. They do not want you that they will charge $131.80 to your account. I didn’t find the app useful at all and stopped using it. Then I opened my bank account today to find they charged me a subscription fee without being listed as a subscription under my settings. Now I literally cannot get food to eat bc of this. It’s not useful and their billing practices are unethical.

title: Hassle to cancel
body: The website says no questions asked if you cancel. This is definitely not true. I sent an image of my payment receipt and they said they couldn’t find my account under my name or email. I accidentally gave the wrong email address and they closed my support ticket when I was still trying to prove my purchase for a refund. It’s been days of back and forth so far trying to get a refund.

title: Amazing
body: I wish this was baked into the iPhone, fixed all my sleep issues and groggy wake ups. Thankful! 🙏

title: Garbage App from the get go …
body: They know what the issue is. Look at other reviews.  Requires access code they email you but doesnt arrive.  Says check junk but nothing there and no other way of accessing customer service the app.  Even the switch to another account button doesnt work.  How do these apps stay in business

title: Best sleep app
body:
I’ve had insomnia for two years. I have tried 5 or 6 other solutions that claimed they could help me. Stellar sleep is the only thing that actually worked for me because of the psychological component that helped me discover how burned out I was and how that was affecting me. I like the videos and the approach and I only wish there was an easier way to go back and search for specific things because I want to rewatch a few specific parts.

Overall highly recommend this app. It’s worked super well for me!

title: Access cod???
body: Where do you get the access code?  I’ve tried logging in every way available but I’m not getting the access code necessary.

title: I’m believer!
body: Stellar sleep has quietly changed my life. I like the emphasis on building skills that I can use to tackle my specific sleep issues vs other apps I’ve tired which try to just clam you down with generic seeming meditation. I’ve found that a lot of my sleep problems are made worse by my anxiety, and Stellar sleep has helped me recognize that and handle it in a way where it no longer affects my sleep. More importantly the app has helped me believe  that I can sleep like a normal person again instead of having to struggle with sleep for the rest of my life. Every day I’m feeling much, much more hopeful about living a normal life with normal sleep.

title: Scam
body: I’m not familiar with this app, but it keeps trying to take $131 from my bank account. If there is a ‘Contact Us’ link to attempt to dispute the charge, I can’t find it.

title: Effective until it stopped working
body:
Works surprising well. Went from an average of 70% to 95% sleep efficiency and more predictable sleep pattern in 4 weeks. Suggested Improvements: 1. Customer support in the app, so I don’t have to post here to contact developer for the following: 2. One place to see (list) all the modules I’ve completed, so I can easily refer back to them. 3. Let me copy the text of the modules so I can add the information to my own Notes.

Edit: dropped from 4 starts to 2. After 40 nights of logged sleep, I can’t access “Sleep Log”. When I tap on it, the app freezes.

title: Helpful but expensive
body: I’m 26 days into the program and it says I’m done.  It has given me some useful ideas & tools.  But I don’t understand why you must pay for 3 months when the program content stops at 26 days.  I’ll have to update this review when I know what happens in month 2 & 3.  Even the content in the first month is pretty repetitive.  I just don’t feel like it was worth $130.

title: Broken system
body:
Firstly, the app is not free. There is no content or benefit before you pay, and this is not clear from the App Store page. 

Secondly, even if you do try to pay, the system breaks and sends you into an endless loop of a broken web page and an app that asks you a lot of meaningless questions. 

It all feels like a scam. Avoid this app.

title: What does this cost?????
body: Have just spent nearly 30 minutes trying to find out the cost for this app.  I don’t want to fill out your sleep questionnaire or give my personal information until I know the cost.  It looks interesting, but several reviews have mentioned that it is “pricey”.  Could someone please define “pricey” for me since the developers don’t seem to include that information or indicate anywhere that there are in-app purchases.

title: Really, actually works
body:
I found Slumber One at a point in my life where I hadn’t slept for weeks. I mean that literally. I track my sleep and I had gone for at least 3 weeks where Fitbit told me I literally had slept 0 minutes and 0 hours in the past 3 weeks.

I tried melatonin. I tried cutting out all coffee. I tried reading a book in bed. I tried meditation. I tried weed. Nothing worked.

Some days I would get tired. So frickin tired that I thought for sure I would be able to sleep that night. Nope. “Fooled you” my brain would say. I would lay in bed so exhausted that I felt like I could die, but I just wouldn’t fall asleep.

When I found Slumber One, I bought it on impulse, but I honestly didn’t think it would work. But I had already spent hundreds of dollars buying things so why not try something else. They’re from Harvard. Maybe it’ll work. If not I can tell people even Harvard can’t help me.

The first week using Slumber One I didn’t think much was changing. More money down the drain. But then the second week, by some miracle I actually slept. I slept for 2 nights. My Fitbit counter was finally no longer at 0. Third week I slept for 5 nights. By the fourth week, I actually slept every single night.

If you’re reading this and you can’t sleep, then do yourself a favor and get this app. This really, actually works. I don’t know how. Some of the stuff they tell you to do just seems completely illogical, but somehow, through their sequence of instructions, I can sleep again.

title: Absolutely fantastic app
body: I used to sleep 4 hrs a night and now I sleep like baby.

title: Avoid
body: I do hate these so-called purveyors of self-self who are in reality thieves waiting for you to trip up and steal £170.00 from you. I cancelled on the day the trial was over as the app did not suit, and they have still stolen £170 from me.  Many more negative reviews to follow

title: Helped sleep - but overpriced
body:
I had tried many things but having this app with everything laid out for me made my realise how poor my sleep hygiene was & moved things in the right direction for me. I sleep much better having used this app.

The price however is absolutely extortionate given that it only lasts 3 months. If it was a lifetime purchase this would be 5 stars.

title: Game changer
body: I’ve been doing this programme for a week. After 3 years of progressively worsening sleep, things are finally starting to move in the right direction. It’s early days but I feel so much less stressed about sleep and am starting to wake less. I’m hopeful things will get better and better!

title: Brilliant!
body: Still my favourite app to go to during periods of sleeplessness!

title: Complete scam
body: Do not download this app. It has very little of value, then once you sign up and cancel within the free trial period they will still charge you £78!!!! Disgusting behaviour

title: Intentional scam
body: Their tactic is to hide their subscription from the Apple subscriptions section in your iPhone, meaning it is significantly more difficult to cancel the subscription (you have to do it through PayPal which is more complicated and easier to forget about).  In my view they are intentionally trapping sleep deprived / vulnerable people into paying an unintentional and extortionate fee for an app which they have probably never even used.

title: Why are you still charging me?
body: I cancelled my subscription via the app before my second extortionate quarterly payment as, quite frankly, once you go through the first month there is no point having the app, even the daily tops don’t appear any more. Total rip off. Then, 6 months down the line I realise that cancelling on the app hadn’t actually cancelled my PayPal payment and they have stolen £300 from me. This just seems to be an exploitation scheme. PayPal dispute incoming.

title: Subscription.
body: Why are you still charging me when I cancelled my subscription months ago ?

title: Overpriced
body:
Although full of useful information, there is nothing in this app that you cannot find for free on the Internet. Like the advice given on so many cure insomnia sites on the Internet, the method involves sleep restriction.
 They  have a rather sneaky ploy of blocking your access to the app the day you cancel your subscription even if it has two months to run. Unless you cancel the day before your subscription expires you lose access to the app you have paid for.

title: Bit of a scam
body: Very helpful at first but when you start the 3 month pay period the quality of things on the app just goes down. The daily tasks don’t come up anymore and becomes pointless ? I would save your money and go elsewhere

title: Extortionate subscription fee
body: I initially was hesitant to pay subscription fee, but decided that it was worth a try to help with sleep. I thought the $188 was for a 12 month subscription fee. It turns out this is for only 3 months, and you automatically pay a further $188. I do not think this APP is worth this extortionate fee, and would probably be cheaper paying for a therapist!

title: Great app! Really like the graphics :)
body: I downloaded the app and really liked the UI.

title: Routine setting
body: I loved the relax section with music and meditations. Lots of useful strategies to get a sleep rhythm. I felt like someone was with me on my journey using this app

title: Life altering ♥️
body:
I’ve just completed a fully dedicated 70 days with this app. Throughout the experience I felt supported. The cognitive behavioural therapy took me well-beyond any sleep hygiene articles so that I could get to the root of my concerns and begin to do my sleep-life differently.

Prior to this I was averaging about 5.5 hours a night. This had been the case for more than 10 years. A combination of being unable to get to sleep to mid-sleep wakings sometimes left me with less than 5 hours - usually at least once a week.

The app gave me permission to go to bed later, and wake up at a rigid time. No weekend sleep ins. (I won’t share any more of their tricks.)

Bottom line, my average is now 7, and I’m happy. Going to bed has become a joy, and my husband now joins me on the couch every morning to share quiet coffee time. It’s been a noon for our household, and I know I will continue to sleep well. 😴

title: Amazing program!
body: I used to get anxious hours before bed, fearful of another sleepless night. This program provided me with the tools to combat these fears and return to a restful, restorative sleep cycle. I highly recommend this for any suffering from insomnia.

title: Best solution for beating insomnia
body: Stellar Sleep is absolutely amazing. I suffered from severe chronic insomnia with virtually completely sleepless nights. Within a week I had already noticed significant improvements.

title: Cured insomnia - life changing
body: After 9 months of waking up in the middle of the night and being unable to go back to sleep for hours, I’m finally sleeping through the night!  It wasn’t easy and didn’t happen right away, but after a month I started to see results.  During the first few weeks I reverted to old (bad) habits a number of times, but continued to make changes and stuck with the program.  I can’t believe how much more energy I have and how much happier I am.  I had even previously tried sleep medication, which didn’t work, and this program is WAY better.  I am so grateful I found this!

title: Completely changed my life, great customer service too
body:
Have only been using for about 2 weeks, but I sleep better than ever. Actually, this is the first time my fear of sleep is starting to go away. The methods are surprising, but extremely effective. The customer service (Jennifer) is fast and more helpful than any other internet service I’ve dealt with. 
Highly recommend, try it for the free week and see.

title: Good advice and nice animations
body: This app is enjoyable so far. Helpful (albeit somewhat basic) tips for better sleep with sleep tracking and background information that leads you to better understand the reasons behind your insomnia. Given my history of insomnia over decades, I wish I had access to this app when I was younger when the impact may have been greater. Still, happy so far with it. The animal icons are also nice. Thanks to the developers!

title: Rip off
body:
You have to pay $180 and you’re not allowed to revisit material?!! Imagine paying the publisher every time you reread a passage in a book!!! 

Some good content on here - find it elsewhere free of charge by googling CBT sleep techniques / you tube relaxation methods ….

title: Better off just seeing a psychologist
body: Waste of money if you have a psychologist.  If you don’t, you could get a few sessions for the same price as a two month subscription if you’re in Australia

title: Cancel your subscription for a massive discount
body:
Go to Profile > Manage Subscription > Cancel now. Select “too expensive” as the reason and receive a massive discount. 

In saying that I still ended up cancelling my subscription. You have to watch each video or you can’t see the next ones. You can’t rewatch previously viewed videos either.

title: Great to start then ….!
body: First week was interesting and useful but since paying the money - nothing basically. Cannot recommend.

title: Expensive for what it is - there are better options
body:
At first this app appeared to have a much better user interface, and far more interactive content than other sleep apps. After paying $3 for the trial week I was happy to pay AUD$189 for another 3 months, as I was optimistic it was going to guide me through a big change in my sleep. Unfortunately, the interactive content and learning materials dropped right back after I forked out the money for the full program. I also found the guided meditations to have very harsh accents that weren’t at all relaxing.

Overall the CBTi approach that this app delivers, alongside the sleep restriction, has been a huge help for my sleep. However, I don’t think the premium on this app is worth it. The Insomnia Coach app developed by the US department of veteran's affairs provides very similar CBTi information as written material, and the sleep diary takes in a lot more detailed sleep information such as how long it takes you to get out of bed. The user interface of this app is basic and it requires a bit more self motivation to get through the program but it is FREE, even if you aren’t a veteran and don’t live in the US. You can also combine this app with other free meditation apps to find what works for you.

I am also really disappointed that I couldn’t leave a review through Google Play store and given there are no other reviews on there it seems a bit suspicious. I downloaded the app on my iPad purely to give this feedback.

title: Scam
body: This app literally charged me $182 twice without consulting me.

title: Scam vibes
body: I don’t understand why this app doesn’t have any other negative reviews. Not only was I suddenly charged a $260 without any notice in the app or elsewhere, the customer support has been very poor. I requested for my account to be shut down and for a refund as per their policy on their website - this was a week ago now - and all I got back was “do you want to go on a payment plan”. When I asked again to cancel the account and for a refund as per the policy I got absolutely nothing back. I didn’t even get a chance to see whether the app was any good for my sleep before the whopping charge has kept me up stressing! Be wary of this app.

title: BEWARE
body: SCAM it will not let you cancel your subscription just fyi. Even if you ‘cancel,’ it doesn’t actually do it. What a horrible app.

title: Really works
body: I was doubtful during the first week of the Stella sleep program and had accepted the fact that I was going to be a poor sleeper my hole life. 3 months later I’m off my sleeping pills and no longer have crushing anxiety about going to bed.

title: Good app but can’t bill properly
body: Do not use. They say they’ll bill you $9 but they bill the $188… in my case twice. And like any app it’s impossible to get a person to reply and help you. Sorry stellar but you have to get your billing sorted. Otherwise yr just another scammer.

title: Scam alert
body: I was charged for a 3 month subscription without selecting one.

title: This app works!
body: Stop your worrying and get this app! Money well spent! Halfway through the program and my average sleep time went up from 5 hours or less to around 5 hours 45 minutes or more, and on track to increase. I wish I learned about cbt-i earlier and found this app earlier! Thanks Slumber One team! Your customer support has been amazing as well!
